R0-normalize.name: Backlog normalization and readiness lint
R0-normalize.purpose: Make Beads itself delivery-grade: consistent ready counts, release/lane labels, missing acceptance criteria patched, and hidden blockers explicit.
R0-normalize.exit: Every active bead has a delivery release, lane, readiness grade, proof lane, and either acceptance criteria or a deliberate horizon/spec status.

A-trust-floor.name: Trust floor
A-trust-floor.purpose: Prevent false evidence, unsafe byte deletion, unsafe daemon/capture access, and unsafe agent-written memory.
A-trust-floor.exit: Full verification classified; security negative tests pass; missing bytes classified; numbers/time/prose-mined fields carry honest provenance; agent writes land as candidates.

B-storage-rebuild-bytes.name: Storage, rebuild, and byte-integrity floor
B-storage-rebuild-bytes.purpose: Make durable/derived storage and blob handling safe at real archive scale.
B-storage-rebuild-bytes.entry: Trust-floor blob/security blockers either closed or explicitly isolated.
B-storage-rebuild-bytes.exit: Blue-green derived rebuilds cannot show partial archives as ready; restore drill passes; blob refs resolve or carry classified missing state.

C-read-evidence-contract.name: One read contract and first-class evidence objects
C-read-evidence-contract.purpose: Unify query/projection/render semantics and make query runs, result sets, findings, and citations addressable.
C-read-evidence-contract.exit: CLI, daemon, MCP, Python API, web, reports, and docs read through the same contract; content-hash citations expose drift states.

D-agent-context-coordination.name: Agent context, memory, and coordination
D-agent-context-coordination.purpose: Make Polylogue useful while agents are working, with safe memory boundaries and scheduler-mediated context.
D-agent-context-coordination.entry: Daemon security and agent-write safety closed.
D-agent-context-coordination.exit: Hooks install; MCP roles/prompts discoverable; context scheduler emits ledgers; two-agent worktree proof exists.

E-variants-preferences.name: Content variants, preferences, and transformed views
E-variants-preferences.purpose: Support translations, simplifications, summaries, and preferences without confusing transformed text with original evidence.
E-variants-preferences.entry: Evidence refs and agent-write safety available for source alignment and candidate review.
E-variants-preferences.exit: Variant refs/nodes/alignment/storage exist; reader/query/projection can show source, variant, and side-by-side views honestly.

F-lineage-compaction.name: Lineage truth and compaction recovery
F-lineage-compaction.purpose: Represent physical/logical sessions, shared prefixes, subagents, compactions, truncation, and regrounding truthfully.
F-lineage-compaction.entry: Origin identity and read consistency contracts available.
F-lineage-compaction.exit: Shared content is stored/counted once, compaction loss is queryable, and regrounding packs pass through the context scheduler.

G-live-performance.name: Live intake, capture, daemon, and interactive performance
G-live-performance.purpose: Make capture/live surfaces observable, fast, bounded, and testable.
G-live-performance.exit: Named SLOs and regression gates exist; daemon push/live cache paths work; capture reliability is visible and tested.

H-web-cockpit.name: Web evidence cockpit
H-web-cockpit.purpose: Turn the web UI into an evidence workbench: baskets, reports, replay, timelines, live tail, long-session navigation.
H-web-cockpit.entry: Read/evidence contract and daemon push state available.
H-web-cockpit.exit: Evidence basket to citable export works; web UI shows stale/partial/degraded states instead of pretending readiness.

I-analytics-experiments.name: Analytics, experiments, and measured learning
I-analytics-experiments.purpose: Answer outcome/cost/process questions with sample frames, uncertainty, and construct-validity limits.
I-analytics-experiments.entry: Evidence honesty, query identity, and measure registry foundations exist.
I-analytics-experiments.exit: Measures are registered, experiments are first-class, analytics render caveats and evidence tiers.

J-embeddings-retrieval.name: Embeddings and semantic retrieval
J-embeddings-retrieval.purpose: Make vector search provider-general, budgeted, measurable, and useful for context retrieval.
J-embeddings-retrieval.entry: Query contracts and context scheduler exist.
J-embeddings-retrieval.exit: FTS/vector/hybrid quality evals exist; local/cloud providers share one interface; large sessions bound vector work.

K-interop-origin-export.name: Interop, origin breadth, and export
K-interop-origin-export.purpose: Import more origins through declared contracts and export citable evidence back out.
K-interop-origin-export.exit: Each origin has detector/parser/fixtures/fidelity docs; Polylogue export/import is content-hash idempotent; outbound citations preserve provenance.

L-external-legibility.name: External legibility, demos, and launch
L-external-legibility.purpose: Make a stranger understand, run, verify, and cite Polylogue.
L-external-legibility.entry: Claims ledger, demos, install proof, and evidence report substrate available.
L-external-legibility.exit: README first screen is clear; one-command demo passes; public claims ledger covers launch claims; cold-reader proof passes.

M-substrate-consolidation.name: Substrate consolidation and codebase simplification
M-substrate-consolidation.purpose: Remove duplicate storage/read/surface code after the contracts they should collapse onto are stable.
M-substrate-consolidation.entry: Read/evidence and storage/rebuild contracts stable enough to refactor around.
M-substrate-consolidation.exit: Storage twins collapse behind a clear boundary; public models are frozen; dead abstractions are deleted or adopted.

N-horizon.name: Horizon and vision work
N-horizon.purpose: Keep speculative or late-gated work visible without letting it distort the safety and proof spine.
N-horizon.entry: Earlier foundation releases stable, or a decision memo explicitly pulls one item forward.
N-horizon.exit: Each item either has an implementation-grade spec or remains parked with a decision record.
